briefs.title: BC Tightens EV Range Rules and Credit System for Auto Suppliers Starting 2026
briefs.summary: BC has updated its Zero-Emission Vehicles Regulation, changing the minimum driving range (how far a vehicle can travel on a charge) required for electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles to qualify for credits under the province's clean car rules. Battery-electric and fuel-cell vehicles now need at least 150 km of range (down from 241 km), while plug-in hybrids must meet new range thresholds that increase from 16 km for 2026–2028 models to 80 km for 2029 and later models. The credit issuance rules for vehicle suppliers also change on October 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: If you supply or sell new vehicles in BC, review updated ZEV range thresholds and credit rules before October 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC ZEV credit issuance new rules take effect for vehicle suppliers
